clinical_criteria: At least one ICD9/ICD-10 code for Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) for filter criteria
At least 3 diagnosis codes for any ICD9/10 code for data floor
30 days or more between first and last date of diagnosis code
At least one diagnosis code after '1/1/2005'
At least one clinical note in the electronic health record (EHR)
Specificity held at >95% for case classification
MGB cutoff of 0.593 for case/non-case status determination
algorithm_summary: This is a Type 2 Diabetes phenotype algorithm that uses a logistic regression classifier trained on chart-reviewed gold standard data. The algorithm first applies filter criteria requiring at least one ICD9/ICD-10 code for T2DM, then applies data floor requirements (≥3 diagnosis codes, ≥30 days between first/last codes, ≥1 code after 2005, ≥1 clinical note). Features include coded diagnosis variables (COD_DX_T2DM, COD_DX_T1DM) and diabetes medication variables (COD_MED_diabetes) derived from multiple RxNorm CUIs and NDC codes for various diabetes medication classes including alpha-glucosidase inhibitors and DPP-IV inhibitors. The model uses LASSO penalized logistic regression with a threshold optimized for >95% specificity to classify cases vs. non-cases, achieving 90-94% positive predictive value across validation sets.
